commit.message:
User/yashasg/beatmap improvement (#19)

* fix: derive rhythm positions from song_time, not accumulated dt

Applied the key insight from fizzd's LD48 rhythm game postmortem:
'Every object that needs to be in sync should do so using only the
song position, and NOT anything else.'

scroll_system: rhythm obstacles (with BeatInfo) now compute position
as SPAWN_Y + (song_time - spawn_time) * scroll_speed instead of
accumulating pos.y += vel.dy * dt each frame. This eliminates
floating-point drift that causes obstacles to slowly desync from the
beat grid over time. Non-rhythm entities (particles, popups, freeplay
obstacles) still use dt-based movement.

shape_window_system: window_timer is now derived from
song_time - window_start instead of accumulated dt. Phase transitions
increment window_start by the exact phase duration (not the current
song_time), following the 'never update your reference point
arbitrarily — only increment by set amounts' rule.

Tests updated to advance song_time instead of relying on dt passthrough.

All 383 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use RenderTexture letterbox in Emscripten path to prevent squish

The Emscripten main loop was rendering directly to the screen canvas
(BeginDrawing → render_system → EndDrawing) while the native path
rendered to a 720×1280 RenderTexture then blitted it letterboxed.
This caused the game to stretch/squish to whatever size the browser
canvas was.

Now both paths use the same RenderTexture → DrawTexturePro letterbox
pipeline. The LoopState struct carries the RenderTexture handle so
the Emscripten callback can access it.

Also simplified CSS: removed media queries in favor of a single rule
(max-width:100vw; max-height:100vh; object-fit:contain) that works
for both mobile and desktop since the letterboxing is handled in C++.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: rewrite level_designer with music-driven obstacle assignment

Rewrote level_designer.py based on research from 5argon's notecharting
guide, DDRKirby's Rhythm Quest devlog, and Liang et al.'s PCG paper.

Architecture changed from combo-rotation to 3-phase pipeline:
  Phase 1 (SELECT): density from song structure + flux threshold.
    Repeated section types reuse the same relative beat pattern
    so players experience 'second time = mastery'.
  Phase 2 (ASSIGN): onset passes determine obstacle type directly.
    kick-only → high_bar, hihat-only → low_bar, snare → lane_block,
    multi-pass/melody → shape_gate. Variety injection forces non-shape
    obstacles after consecutive runs, preventing monotony.
  Phase 3 (CLEAN): safety net removes violations (unchanged rules).
    Added gap monotony cleaner: no single gap value > 40%.

Results vs old generator:
  Gap monotony: 20-35% (was 41-47%)
  Hard variety: 73% shape_gate + 20% lane + 6% bars (was 60/32/4)
  Pattern reuse: verse 1 and verse 2 share same beat pattern
  Section-aware: bridge=sparse, drop=dense+consistent, intro=minimal

Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json with new generator.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add --difficulty CLI param (easy/medium/hard)

Usage:
  ./build/shapeshifter --difficulty easy
  ./build/shapeshifter --difficulty hard --test-player pro
  ./run.sh --difficulty easy

Defaults to 'medium' when not specified. The difficulty parameter
is passed to load_beat_map() which selects the matching difficulty
tier from the beatmap JSON.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: disable jump/slide — game focuses on shape + lane only

Removed jump (swipe up / W) and slide (swipe down / S) from active
gameplay. The game was too complex with 3 simultaneous mechanics
(shape matching + lane dodging + vertical movement).

Changes:
- player_action_system: removed jump/slide gesture handling
- level_designer: removed low_bar/high_bar from all difficulty tiers
  and section roles
- Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json (shape_gate + lane_block only)
- Updated tests to verify jump/slide are disabled

Engine code for VerticalState/VMode remains intact (dead code) so
the feature can be re-enabled later if desired.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: flow-based shape picker replaces frequency-based assignment

Shape selection now driven by playability flow, not onset pass names.
Based on 5argon/DDRKirby research: 'accuracy to the song is less
important than how fun it feels to play.'

New rules (all in Phase 2 generation, no overlap with Phase 3 cleanup):
1. Section palette: intro/bridge = 1 shape (center lane only),
   verse = 2 shapes (circle+square), pre-chorus/drop = all 3.
   This creates a natural difficulty ramp as shapes are introduced.
2. Short gaps (≤2 beats): repeat same shape (groove/L1-kick principle).
   Player stays locked in rhythm without disruption.
3. Long gaps (3+ beats): switch to different shape from palette.
   Player has time to prepare; variety feels earned.
4. Anti-triple: built into picker, never 3 of same in a row.

Removed dead code: PASS_TO_SHAPE dict, dominant_pass() function.

Regenerated 1_stomper_beatmap.json. Intro is now square-only,
verses use 2 shapes, drops use all 3.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: increase obstacle density and tile patterns across long sections

The beatmap felt too empty — hard mode had 0.27 obstacles/beat with
gaps up to 22 beats (9 seconds of nothing). Two causes:

1. Double-filtering: flux threshold removed 25% of events, then
   section density removed another 20-50%. Raised section densities
   (verse 0.50→0.65, drop 0.80→0.90) and difficulty scales
   (easy 0.50→0.55, medium 0.75→0.80). Lowered flux thresholds
   (hard 25→15 percentile).

2. Pattern stretching: when a 5.7s pre-chorus pattern was reused
   for a 29.7s section, it stretched across 5x the length leaving
   huge gaps. Now tiles the pattern to fill longer sections (e.g.
   29.7s section gets 5 repetitions of the 5.7s pattern).

Results:
  easy:   92→121 obs, 0.7 presses/sec, max gap 18 (intro)
  medium: 100→134 obs, 0.7 presses/sec, max gap 12
  hard:   123→153 obs, 0.8 presses/sec, max gap 13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: increase hard mode density to ~1 press/sec

Hard: scale 1.00→1.20, flux threshold p15→p5.
Now 171 obstacles at 0.9 presses/sec (was 153 at 0.8/sec).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: lower flux thresholds + skip CI on content/tools changes in PRs

Flux thresholds lowered: easy 70→50, medium 40→25. More musical
events survive filtering, giving denser levels:
  easy:   121→115 obs (slightly fewer due to event selection)
  medium: 134→150 obs (0.33 presses/sec)
  hard:   171 obs unchanged

CI: added paths-ignore to pull_request triggers in all 4 workflows.
Changes to content/, tools/, design-docs/, *.md, *.log no longer
trigger builds on PRs. Push to main still builds everything.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: preserve window shortening and late-spawn clamp under song-time timing

- collision_system: adjust window_start backward instead of advancing
  window_timer when scale < 1.0; shape_window_system re-derives
  window_timer from song_time - window_start every frame so the old
  mutation was silently discarded on the next tick.
- beat_scheduler_system: when late-spawn clamping fires, store an
  adjusted effective_spawn_time in BeatInfo so scroll_system
  reproduces the clamped start_y via its song-time formula.
- Update two existing window_scaling tests to assert window_start
  movement instead of window_timer advancement.
- Add four new tests: BAD/Perfect timing collision, clamped spawn
  time in BeatInfo.

commit.message:
fix(browser): remove PLAYWRIGHT_CLI_SESSION from daemon env to fix ENOENT

The playwright-core startDaemon() function passes PLAYWRIGHT_CLI_SESSION
as --endpoint to the daemon child process, which interprets 'delegate'
as a browser WebSocket endpoint to connect to — causing 'connect ENOENT
delegate'. Fix: strip the env var and pass session name via -s flag.

Also set cwd to COPILOT_WORK_DIR for consistent workspace detection
(matching copilot dispatch), and make git set-url non-fatal.

commit.message:
fix: garbled output on POSIX/C locale systems

On fresh VPS installs the system locale is often C or POSIX (ASCII
only). UTF-8 bytes for symbols like ✔ ⚠ ✘ ── and Chinese text are
output as raw multi-byte sequences, appearing as garbled characters
(乱码) in the terminal.

Fix: add _setup_locale() called before set -euo pipefail that picks
the best available UTF-8 locale in order: C.UTF-8 → en_US.UTF-8 →
bare LANG=en_US.UTF-8 fallback.  C.UTF-8 is available on all modern
glibc systems (Debian/Ubuntu/RHEL 8+) and is preferred because it
avoids pulling in full locale data.

Also add DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive to apt-get calls to suppress
interactive locale-related prompts during dependency installation.

Bump version to 0.2.7

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add docs users and notifications scaffolds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Rework fromiter() with direct chunked construction + Phase 3 optimisations

Phase 1: Replace lazyudf + NamedTemporaryFile + reshape path with a
direct, single-pass chunked construction:
- c_order=True / 1-D: np.fromiter into a flat buffer, then dst[:] = buf.reshape(shape)
- c_order=False: consume the iterator chunk-by-chunk via iterchunks_info()
- Fix latent range-restart bug: call iter(iterable) up-front so re-iterable
  objects (range, list) don't restart on each chunk call
- Remove unused tempfile import; add islice to itertools imports

Phase 2: Tests and docstring
- Add 12 new tests covering single-pass consumption, generator no-rewind,
  chunk-value correctness, exhausted-iterator error, empty shape, structured
  dtype, higher-dim (3-D, 4-D) shapes
- Update docstring to document new single-pass semantics

Phase 3A: Page buffer for c_order=False
- Pre-read _PAGE_NELEMS (1 M) elements per page to reduce np.fromiter
  calls from O(n_chunks) to O(total / page_size)
- Handles tail compaction for correct behaviour at page boundaries

Phase 3C: NumPy ndarray fast path
- isinstance(iterable, np.ndarray) check before any iteration logic
- Falls through to np.asarray + reshape + dst[:] = arr, bypassing all
  Python-level iteration (~63x faster for numpy array inputs)
- Add 6 parametrized tests for the fast path
- Add fast-path timing to benchmark bonus section

Benchmark: bench/ndarray/fromiter.py
- 4 sections: chunk allocation/reuse, traversal order, on-disk vs in-memory,
  large-array headline throughput
- Bonus section shows numpy fast-path (opt C) achieving 4+ GB/s vs
  ~0.07 GB/s for generator inputs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: Reality private key empty — use full path for xray x25519

gen_reality_keys() was calling 'xray x25519' without the full path
/usr/local/bin/xray.  On Debian/Ubuntu root shells /usr/local/bin is
often absent from PATH, so the command failed silently (stderr was
redirected to /dev/null), leaving private_key/public_key as empty
strings in state.json.  Xray then rejected the config with
'empty privateKey' (exit code 23).

Fixes:
- gen_reality_keys: use /usr/local/bin/xray x25519 (full path)
- gen_reality_keys: switch awk '{print $3}' to '{print $NF}' to
  handle any whitespace variation in xray output
- gen_reality_keys: validate extracted keys are non-empty and print
  the raw output on failure for easier debugging
- setup_vless_reality: add '|| return 1' after gen_reality_keys so
  the setup aborts immediately instead of writing a broken config
- generate_config_vless_reality: validate private_key non-empty
  before generating config, giving a clear actionable error message

Bump version to 0.2.6

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: service port binding, duplicate user index, and index lookup

- install_xray_service: move to /etc/systemd/system/ for cross-distro
  compatibility; add AmbientCapabilities=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE so the
  xray user can bind privileged ports (443 etc.) without root; remove
  ProtectSystem=strict sandbox that blocked config/cert writes; chown
  config and log dirs to xray user after service install

- user management: all 7 setup_* functions now check for existing users
  before creating the initial admin and prompt to clear them, preventing
  duplicate index=1 entries when re-deploying a protocol

- user management: all 6 index-lookup calls (del_user, toggle_user,
  reset_user_uuid, traffic_show_user, traffic_set_limit,
  traffic_reset_user) pipe through head -1 to safely handle any
  pre-existing duplicate indexes

- add repair_user_indexes() utility that detects duplicate indexes in
  an existing users.json and renumbers all users sequentially by
  created_at, making it possible to recover broken installs

- bump version to 0.2.5

* refactor: implement architecture improvements from DoD review

1. Split rhythm.h into three headers by access pattern:
   - beat_map.h: loaded-once data (BeatEntry, BeatMap) — includes <string>, <vector>
   - song_state.h: runtime singletons (SongState, HPState, SongResults)
   - rhythm.h: per-entity components (TimingGrade, BeatInfo) + timing helpers
   rhythm.h re-exports both for backward compatibility.

2. Extract begin_shape_window() in player_action_system to eliminate
   the duplicated 9-line window-init block (Idle and Active+different
   branches were identical).

3. Extract scene-draw helpers from render_system (456→~450 lines but
   now 5 focused functions): draw_title_scene, draw_hud,
   draw_game_over_overlay, draw_song_complete_overlay, draw_pause_overlay.

4. Move enter_playing() into setup_play_session() (play_session.h/cpp),
   separating entity construction from the game state machine.

5. Single-pass collision: replace 5 separate entt::view iterations with
   one view + switch on Obstacle::kind. Simpler code, one iteration.

All 383 tests pass, zero behavior change.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: split PlayerShape into PlayerShape + ShapeWindow

PlayerShape (12B) now holds only render-path data: current, previous,
morph_t. ShapeWindow (28B) holds rhythm-mode timing data: target_shape,
phase_raw, window_timer, window_start, peak_time, window_scale, graded.

Systems that only need the current shape (render_system,
player_movement_system) no longer pull in 28 bytes of window timing
data. Systems that manage the window (shape_window_system,
collision_system, player_action_system) query both components.

Also removed spurious PlayerShape from burnout_system's view — it
queried the component but never read any field from it.

All 383 tests pass (803 assertions).

修复:
- [P0] get_active_clients 添加 email 字段 — 修复流量统计完全无效的致命 bug
- [P0] 修复 acme.sh cron 续期路径 (acme-renew.sh → acme.sh --renew-all)
- [P1] rebuild_config 使用 tmp+mv 原子写入，防止配置清空
- [P1] traffic_collect 修复 pipefail 下的管道安全问题
- [P1] add_user 合并 trojan/ss 密码为一次 jq 写入
- [P1] write_config 添加空文件检查
- gen_user_link 错误消息输出到 stderr，避免命令替换捕获
- show_links 链接生成失败时优雅降级

增强:
- setup_shadowsocks 支持自定义用户名和流量限额
- menu_service 添加 while 循环
- menu_users 显示当前协议和用户数
- do_uninstall 三选项（完全卸载/保留数据/取消）+ 二次确认
- restore_config 恢复前校验（xray run -test / jq empty）
- traffic_monthly_check 补偿机制（服务器停机错过重置日）
- TLS 协议链接生成域名为空时报错而非回退 IP
- 主菜单 IP 缓存，避免每次刷新 curl
- validate_reset_day 集中验证（1-28）
- gb_to_bytes 数字输入验证
- 版本升级至 v0.2

fix: use SynopticPy package name in requirements (#8)

* fix: use SynopticPy package name in requirements

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use SynopticPy package name in pyproject.toml too

requirements.txt was already corrected in 44db24a; pyproject.toml had
the same wrong dep (`synoptic>=0.0.2`). Without this, plain
`pip install -e .` resolves to an unrelated 2014 PyPI package called
"synoptic" (an AJAX notes manager whose __init__.py uses Python 2 print
syntax and SyntaxErrors on Python 3.13), clobbering the conda-forge
synopticpy install.

Verified locally: after this change, `pip install -e .` succeeds in the
brc-tools conda env without --no-deps and `from synoptic import services`
still resolves correctly.
